summary: Bird Song brilliance, smoky second set, solid opening
blurb: Opening with a fiery Hell In A Bucket and tight Bird Song, the first set stays tight. The second set builds into a smoking Smokestack Lightning and an intense Louie Louie → Samson jam, highlighted by Jerry’s ripping solo. Overall the show delivers strong mid‑set jams despite some vocal inconsistencies.
review:
April 5, 1988 at the Hartford Civic Center captures the Dead in late‑80s form – a set that kicks off with a blazing Hell In A Bucket and a soaring Bird Song where Jerry’s guitar is described as “ripping” with an unforgettable solo. Bob Weir’s Smokestack Lightning lands like a bolt of lightning into the drums, setting up a second‑set surge that culminates in a ferocious Louie Louie → Samson jam – the kind of high‑energy stretch fans call “intense”.  Brent’s organ lifts the All Over Now groove and adds color to the Louie Louie excursion, while Phil’s gritty bass anchors the Samson & Delilah improvisation.  The night isn’t flawless; vocal delivery wavers on Sugaree and a few numbers, and some moments feel flat, but the overall momentum and tight interplay keep the show firmly in the “solid” camp for this era.

For listeners wanting the clearest window into that night, the Miller soundboard (gd1988-04-05.sbd.miller.91234.sbeok.flac16) offers a warm, rich mix with just enough audience bleed to preserve the live feel while showcasing the band’s tightness.  Whether you’re chasing Jerry’s Bird Song heroics or the second‑set firestorm, this show delivers a rewarding snapshot of the Dead’s late‑80s grind.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Two recordings are explicitly positive, praising Bird Song, Louie Louie → Samson jam and overall tight playing; two others are mixed but still note strong moments without consistent criticism. The balance of positive remarks across multiple sources justifies a moderate‑high sentiment boost.

best_recording.reason: Soundboard source with warm, rich capture; highlights the key moments (Bird Song solo, Smokestack Lightning, Louie Louie → Samson) while maintaining clarity and minimal technical issues.

key_highlights: Smoking Smokestack Lightning into drums that launches the second set
Jerry’s ripping Bird Song solo that soars above the mix
Intense Louie Louie → Samson jam driving the show’s momentum
Strong opening with Hell In A Bucket and solid mid‑set jams

band_performance.Jerry: Ripping guitar work with a standout solo on Bird Song; vocals uneven, weaker on Sugaree and some numbers but improve later in the set.
band_performance.Phil: Gritty bass praised especially during the Samson & Delilah jam, providing solid foundation throughout.
band_performance.Bob: Vocals lack nuance on certain tracks yet deliver punchy power on Smokestack Lightning; drives the energy of the Sampson jam.
band_performance.Brent: Organ lifts on All Over Now and adds confidence on Louie Louie; more adventurous playing than usual.
band_performance.Mickey: Drumming solidly supports the jams, though a stand‑alone Smokestack Lightning transition was noted as feeling ripped off by one reviewer.
band_performance.Billy: Provides furious energy on Samson jam and maintains steady rhythmic drive across both sets.
